passive.name: Hiyokurenri
passive.description: Every time Kanshou specifies a location and activates his flying sword skill, Moya also sends the same number of flying swords to the same location, dealing the same amount of damage. Flying sword skills share a maximum number of uses. When a flying sword hits an enemy, it can inflict sword energy on each enemy. When a flying sword hits a target with sword energy, each stack deals an additional 75 (75 + 12% of magic attack) magic damage. If an enemy is inflicted with multiple sword powers at the same time, subsequent damage will be reduced to 50%. Normal attacks deal 300 (300 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage.

skill1.name: Kenzuka of protection
skill1.description: Summons Kenzuka in the specified direction, dealing 300 (300 + 50% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within range and knocking them back, while retreating in the opposite direction. Enemies hit by Kenzuka have their [ICON_MAGIC_DEFENSE] reduced by 150 for 3 seconds. Passive effect: Gain 1 stack of Kenzuka for each enemy you defeat or assist, and each stack increases magic attack by 15. Up to 20 stacks.

skill2.name: Male and female twin swords, close
skill2.description: Fires a flying sword at the specified location, dealing 325 (325 + 45% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route. Inflicts an additional 75 (75 + 12% of magic attack) magic damage on enemies with sword energy. Flying sword skills have a shared usage limit, and will recover once every 5-6 seconds (reduced depending on skill level). If the same Kenki is activated multiple times at the same time, the damage of the latter will be reduced by 50%.

skill3.name: Male and female twin swords, far
skill3.description: Fires a flying sword at the specified location, dealing 325 (325 + 45% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies along the route. Inflicts an additional 75 (75 + 12% of magic attack) magic damage on enemies with sword energy. Flying sword skills have a shared usage limit, and will recover once every 5-6 seconds (reduced depending on skill level). If the same Kenki is activated multiple times at the same time, the damage of the latter will be reduced by 50%.

skill4.name: Kenrai
skill4.description: Instantly stack the flying sword skill twice, summoning more flying swords and strengthening the next flying sword. Passive: Increases the range of vision by 15% and increases the number of stackable "Female and Male Swords" by 1. Also, each time the flying sword hits an enemy, the cooldown of "Kenrai" is reduced by 1 second.

strengths: Overwhelming burst damage when hitting at the intersection of skills that draw unique trajectories
Ultra-long range that allows for unilateral poke and minion processing from a distance.
weaknesses: The difficulty of hitting the unique curved skill trajectory is extremely high, and the degree of dependence on player skill is extremely high.
They have poor self-defense skills and are extremely vulnerable when approached by assassins etc.
strategy.earlyGame: Has high poke (long-range attack) capability from the early game. Accurately grasp the trajectory of Skill 2 and Skill 3, and apply pressure by chipping away at the enemy mid laner's HP over minions.
strategy.midGame: Magic Attack passively increases infinitely with every kill or assist, so actively participate in teamfights to stack it. Do not approach unwarded bushes carelessly, and use skills from a distance to scout.
strategy.lateGame: His burst damage in the late game is top tier among all mages. If you land a clean hit with Ultimate-enhanced skills on the enemy Marksman or Mage, you can deal fatal damage (or instantly kill them) in one shot.
strategy.teamfight: Always position at the very back and snipe the enemy backline over their frontline. If targeted by an Assassin, use Skill 1 to knock back the enemy, gain distance, and then counterattack.
strategy.commonMistakes: Lacking the aiming skill to land hits at the intersection of skills (where damage is highest), resulting in zero damage output. Also, using the self-defense Skill 1 aggressively, and getting immediately killed by an Assassin right after.

synergy.reason: Because it draws enemies to one place, it is easy to hit multiple people with skill combos.
Provides cooldown reduction and sustain, allowing for constant poke.

counters.reason: With its high mobility and burst damage, it can instantly close the distance and kill you instantly.
He is forcibly engaged with an ult from a distance and is captured before he can escape.

Resetting the cooldown of Piercing Spear when Sacred Procession and Shield of Awe hit an enemy.
Enhanced basic attacks deal an additional 40% damage to non-hero units.

skill3.name: shield of reverence
skill3.description: Hold up a shield and gain a 1200 (1200 + 16% additional physical) shield (CC disabled while active). After 1.5 seconds, counterattack with the holy shield and deal 450 (450 + 150% of additional physical) magic damage to enemies within range. Every time you take damage from an enemy hero while the shield is up, the counterattack damage from the holy shield is increased by 10% and the activation time is reduced by 10% (up to 5 stacks).

Every time you take damage from an enemy hero while the shield is up, the counterattack damage from the holy shield is increased by 10% and the activation time is reduced by 10% (up to 5 stacks).

Move to the nearest friendly tower. When taking off, it deals 600 (600 + 180% of physical attack) physical damage (increases according to hero level) to enemies within range and knocks them up for 0.75 seconds. At the same time, protect surrounding allies and move them together to the tower.
If the resurrection time is less than 10 seconds, active skills cannot be used.
If Skill 3 is activated during death, protection of allies and subsequent effects cannot be applied upon resurrection.

strengths: Possesses multiple movement skills and extremely high mobility to stick to the rear without missing the fleeing enemy.
A unique passive that allows you to move around the map as a spirit even after death, providing vision and buffs to your allies while reviving on the spot.
weaknesses: If you receive CC, your combo will be interrupted, and your durability will not be high, so you will be defeated easily.
Once you jump in, it is difficult to escape on your own, and the risk is high after engagement.
strategy.earlyGame: Her jungling speed in the early game is very fast. Cycle Skill 1 and Skill 2 smoothly to quickly reach level 4. Utilize the shield to gank while maintaining high HP.
strategy.midGame: Using Skill 2 dash as a starting point, the combo of reducing Skill 2's cooldown with Skill 1 and Ultimate to land consecutive dashes (spear thrusts) is powerful. Relentlessly target the enemy Mage or Marksman.
strategy.lateGame: In late-game teamfights, you can play a role in absorbing enemy attacks and CC by diving right into the middle of the enemy formation and deploying the massive shield of your Ultimate. Since you can move as a spirit after death and freely choose your resurrection point, focus on providing vision for your team.
strategy.teamfight: Focus on the enemy carry, quickly close the distance, and deal burst damage. After being killed, move in the true spirit state (soul) to continuously provide vision of the enemy jungle and key enemy heroes, supporting your allies.
strategy.commonMistakes: Using the Ultimate with bad timing, allowing enemies to easily escape its range. Also, not moving to a safe location upon death, resurrecting right in the middle of the enemy formation, and getting killed again immediately.

synergy.reason: Create a kill chance with CC and peel that match the engagement.
Taking advantage of the opening Athena has opened, she can deal powerful follow-up damage.

counters.reason: Engagement can be easily hindered by mobility buffs and silence skills.
With his high mobility and forced movement skills, he can completely miss the target of skill combos.

passive.name: Can't Touch This
passive.description: When Cai Yan takes damage, she gains 35% Movement Speed (decays continuously) for 2s. She also recovers 250 (+50% Magical Attack) Health every second for 2s. This effect can trigger once every 10s.

skill1.name: Healing Hymn
skill1.description: Cai Yan plays a healing hymn, increasing her Movement Speed by 40% while recovering 60 (+20% Magical Attack) Health for herself and nearby ally heroes every 0.5s for 3s (healing on non-hero units is halved).

skill2.name: Earworm
skill2.description: Cai Yan fires a sound wave that bounces between enemies, dealing 270 (+48% Magical Attack) magical damage and stunning them for 0.75s per bounce. The wave can bounce up to 6 times, and up to 2 times on the same target. The second hit on the same target deals 25% damage and stun duration.

skill3.name: Tune of Tranquility
skill3.description: Cai Yan plays the Tune of Tranquility, recovering 90 (+45% Magical Attack) Health every 0.5s for 5s for the nearby ally hero with the lowest Health. She also increases their Physical and Magical Defense by 200 (+30% Magical Attack).

strengths: Unmatched sustain and capability in protracted teamfights thanks to her constant AoE healing.
Excellent protective ability with her Skill 2 bouncing stun and Ultimate's defense and healing buffs.
weaknesses: Her support potential drops significantly if enemies purchase anti-heal items.
Vulnerable to high burst damage that can instantly eliminate her or her allies before she can heal them.
strategy.earlyGame: Stick close to your allied Marksman from the early game, allowing them to lane safely while recovering HP. Cast Skill 2 (bouncing sound wave) near enemy heroes in the minion wave to harass them with stuns.
strategy.midGame: Always move with multiple allies to increase the overall durability of the team. When enemies are grouped up, Skill 2's stun becomes a very powerful disruption, useful for initiating teamfights.
strategy.lateGame: Her healing power becomes extremely high in the late game, but do not be overconfident as enemies often buy anti-heal items. Make sure to never leave the side of your most important carry (the damage-dealing hero).
strategy.teamfight: At the moment your allies' HP drops rapidly, use Ultimate and Skill 1 simultaneously to forcefully keep them alive. Pay close attention to your own positioning so you are not caught in enemy AoE attacks.
strategy.commonMistakes: Playing too aggressively as if allies are invincible, even when enemies have purchased anti-heal items. Also, using Skill 2 when the enemy is alone, preventing the bounce effect from triggering.

synergy.reason: Provides massive healing and peel, creating a safe environment for Hou Yi to stand and deliver high sustained damage.
Significantly boosts Gao Jianli's survivability when diving into the enemy team, maximizing the impact of his Ultimate.

counters.reason: Musashi's Ultimate anti-heal effect completely negates her core strength of sustaining the team.
Her tendency to group up with allies makes the team highly vulnerable to Lady Zhen's AoE freezes and slows.

passive.name: Soul Sacrifice
passive.description: Donghuang creates dark orbs. When an orb touches an enemy, it deals 100 (+50% Magical Attack) plus 3% of the target's Max Health as magical damage. Donghuang also recovers 162 (96 + 40% Magical Attack + 2% Max Health) Health. Hitting non-hero units recovers 1% Max Health. He can have up to 3 dark orbs active at once.

skill1.name: Eclipse
skill1.description: Donghuang unleashes his power, dealing 300 (+40% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies and generating 1 dark orb that orbits him. Max 3 orbs. If this skill hits an enemy, its cooldown and mana cost are reduced by 50%. If he has 3 orbs, he recovers 150 (+60% Magical Attack) Health. The power of Dark Impact scales with the number of orbs.

skill2.name: Dark Impact
skill2.description: Donghuang consumes all his dark orbs (minimum 1) and fires them at a target location. The orbs strike progressively further away, each dealing 500 (+60% Magical Attack) magical damage. Upon casting, his Movement Speed increases by 30% for 1s, plus an extra 10% per orb consumed. The first orb slows enemies by 30% for 1s, the second slows them significantly for 1s, and the third stuns enemies for 1s.

skill3.name: Forsaken Contract
skill3.description: Donghuang suppresses an enemy hero for 2s. During the suppression, Donghuang and the target share all damage taken. Casting this skill instantly grants 3 dark orbs, and their rotation speed increases by 25% during the suppression (scales with magical attack up to 100%).

skill2.name: Nature's Spirit
skill2.description: Guiguzi summons spirits that deal 100 (+15% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s for 2s to nearby enemies. After 2s, the spirits pull enemies toward Guiguzi, stunning them for 1s and dealing 400 (+60% Magical Attack) magical damage. skill3.name: Oracle: Fog

strengths: One of the best initiation tools in the game with his Skill 2's AoE pull and stun.
Ultimate provides team-wide stealth, acceleration, and powerful defense-shredding debuffs.
weaknesses: His pull is useless without follow-up damage from teammates, making communication essential (hard in solo queue).
Must dive into the middle of the enemy team, but lacks the durability of a true tank, making him fragile when focused.

synergy.reason: Guiguzi groups enemies together, allowing Gao Jianli to land devastating AoE damage perfectly.
The pull and stun perfectly sets up Angela to unleash her wide-area burst combo.

counters.reason: Can easily dodge Guiguzi's pull with his invincibility skills and counter-attack with high damage.
Can stop Guiguzi in his tracks with long-range crowd control before he can get close enough to pull.

passive.name: Master Strategist
passive.description: Hitting enemies with active skills grants a stack of strategy marks lasting 10s (max 5 stacks). At 5 stacks, summons 5 magical orbs around himself for 10s that automatically fire at the nearest enemy, dealing 135 (+25% Magical Attack) magical damage each. Prioritizes enemy heroes. Damage is reduced by 40% against monsters and increased by 40% against minions.

skill1.name: Arcane Bolt
skill1.description: Fires 3 arcane bolts in a designated direction, dealing 450 (+70% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in their path. Multiple bolts hitting the same enemy deal 20% damage after the first. Each bolt grants 1 stack of strategy mark. Stores 1 charge every 7.5s (max 2 charges).

skill2.name: Spatial Shift
skill2.description: Blinks in a designated direction, creating a magic field at the start and end points that deals 350 (+52% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies. If an enemy is hit by this damage again within 5s, they are heavily slowed (decays over 3s). Subsequent fields hitting the same enemy deal 50% damage. Stores 1 charge every 7.5s (scales with CDR, max 2 charges).

skill3.name: Winning Strategy
skill3.description: Locks onto a target and charges a spirit bomb. When fired, deals 450 (+55% Magical Attack) magical damage. Enemies in the path or non-hero targets hit take the same damage and are knocked up for 0.5s. The bomb deals 2% more damage for every 1% of the target's missing Health. If the bomb kills an enemy hero, Kongming instantly gains 5 stacks of strategy marks and reduces this skill's cooldown by 80%. If the locked enemy dies before firing, the cooldown is also reduced by 80%.

strengths: High mobility combined with devastating single-target burst damage using his passive.
Excellent at securing multiple kills in teamfights due to his Ultimate's cooldown reset mechanic.
weaknesses: As an assassin-style mage, he is very squishy and vulnerable to crowd control.
Requires close-quarters engagement to build passive stacks, making his playstyle high-risk.
strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, focus on farming in the mid lane and consciously use minions to stack your passive. Harass enemies with Skill 1 while participating in ganks or securing river vision in sync with your allied Jungler's movements.
strategy.midGame: In the mid game when core items start coming together, he shows his true value in small skirmishes. Trigger your passive from quick positioning with Skill 2, and aim to reset cooldowns by finishing off low-HP enemies with your Ultimate.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game when enemy items are also complete, stepping forward carelessly carries the risk of being instantly killed. It is important to wait for your allies to engage and participate in the fight only after confirming enemy CC skills have been consumed.
strategy.teamfight: Never initiate by yourself in teamfights; instead, poke with Skill 1 from the backline. When enemy HP is low, dive in with Skill 2 and aim for continuous kills (kill resets) with your Ultimate to instantly break the enemy formation.
strategy.commonMistakes: Diving right into the middle of the enemy formation with Skill 2 immediately after the fight starts. Also, wasting the Ultimate on full-HP enemies or tanks, failing to utilize the kill reset, which is a common mistake for beginners.

synergy.reason: 強力なCCで敵を確実に拘束することで、孔明のコンボやウルトを安全に叩き込めるため。

counters.reason: 機動力が低い序盤にジャングルに侵入して圧力をかけ、孔明のファームを妨害できるため。
バーストダメージを耐えきり、長時間の張り付きで孔明の機動力を封じることができるため。

passive.name: Eternal River
passive.description: Increases her Movement Speed by 30. If koi fish from 'Koi Leap' or 'Severing Bridge' pass through a water current created by the other skill, it triggers an explosion dealing 140 (+25% Magical Attack) magical damage and slowing Movement Speed by 30% for 1s. Every 250 Magical Attack increases the skill range of 'Koi Leap' and 'Severing Bridge' by 50, up to a maximum of 200.

skill1.name: Koi Leap
skill1.description: Summons a koi fish that swims in the designated direction, dealing 300 (+55% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in its path and knocking them up for 0.5s. It leaves a water current for 2.5s that deals 75 (+12% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s. Casting this skill grants Da Qiao 30% Movement Speed for 2.5s.

skill2.name: Fate of the Sea
skill2.description: Summons a magic circle that lasts for 4s and provides vision. When it expires, it teleports one ally back to the spawn point, cleansing all debuffs and allowing them to return to their original position via the crystal's warp point. If no ally hero is warped, it leaves a mark that recalls the first ally to touch it. Activating again removes the mark. Cooldown is reduced by 50% if no ally hero is warped. Da Qiao gets priority for the warp and instantly recalls if she touches the mark.

skill3.name: Severing Bridge
skill3.description: Releases koi fish from left to right, dealing 300 (+55% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in the path and knocking them up for 0.5s. The koi leaves a water current for 2.5s that deals 75 (+12% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s.

skill4.name: Whirlpool of the Sea
skill4.description: Summons a massive magic circle at a designated location, dealing 500 (+90% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies within. The circle lasts 8s. Allies can tap the Whirlpool portal to teleport to the circle. Da Qiao and allies within the circle gain a 350 (+50% Magical Attack) shield, while Da Qiao gains an extra shield equal to 7% of her bonus Max HP.

strengths: Unmatched map control, able to instantly summon her entire team across the map with her Ultimate.
Skill 2 allows allies to recall, fully heal, and return to the fight almost immediately.
weaknesses: Heavily reliant on team coordination; allies must understand her portals to maximize her potential.
Relies on movement speed buffs for defense and is extremely vulnerable to AoE crowd control and burst damage.
strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, assist the mid laner or Jungler with their farm, using Skill 1 and Skill 3 to help clear waves while harassing the enemy. Secure vision thoroughly and create an environment where allies can move safely.
strategy.midGame: Move across the entire map in the mid game to support allied ganks and retreats. Placing Skill 2 at the feet of low-HP allies to return them safely can significantly increase the overall survival rate of the team.
strategy.lateGame: Coordinating split pushes using the Ultimate, as well as fights around the Tyrant and Overlord, are key. By instantly gathering allies, you can intentionally create a numerical advantage.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, place Skill 2 in a position where allies can easily retreat (slightly backwards), while deploying the Ultimate in a safe location to bring them back to the frontline. Peeling for allies against enemy Assassins with the silence and knock-up from Skills 1 and 3 is also important.
strategy.commonMistakes: Accidentally placing Skill 2 at the feet of allies during combat, unintentionally returning an allied carry to base. Also, deploying the Ultimate right in the middle of enemies or in an unfavorable position, causing the summoned allies to be wiped out.

synergy.reason: His strong basic attacks and CC synergize well with Da Qiao's fast engages and disengages.
Teleporting the team in to back up Mulan's ambushes can completely turn the tide of a teamfight.

counters.reason: Can easily bypass the frontline with his high mobility to assassinate Da Qiao before she can support.
His strong disengage completely nullifies Da Qiao's attempt to force fights with her Ultimate.

passive.name: Artillery Spirit
passive.description: Basic attacks grant 6-12 Physical Attack and 0.75%-1.5% Crit Rate (scales with level) for 1.5s, stacking up to 5 times. In Artillery Mode, this buff is doubled. Switching modes resets the duration and converts the buff to match the new mode.

skill1.name: Chasing Arrow
skill1.description: Huang Zhong gains 70% Movement Speed (decays over 2s). If used while in Artillery Mode, it instantly cancels the mode. Passive: Increases Physical Attack by 20 (doubled while this skill is active).

skill2.name: Mine Trap
skill2.description: Places a mine that grants vision. Enemies touching it take 360 (+80% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage and are revealed for 3s. If the target is a hero, they are slowed by 25% for 2s and suffer 10%-20% Armor Pierce (scales with level), while Huang Zhong gains a 360 (+5.5% Bonus Physical Attack) shield for 3s. Casting speed increases if an enemy is in the area (except in Artillery Mode). Can place up to 2 mines.

skill3.name: Heavy Artillery
skill3.description: Dashes a short distance and deploys his heavy artillery, rooting himself in place. He gains 200 Physical and Magical Defense for 15s, and his basic attacks deal 350 physical damage to enemies in the center (damage is reduced by 40% for enemies outside the center). Artillery mode ends automatically after 5-9 attacks (scales with level). Deals 50% damage to towers. Passive: Entering Artillery mode resets the CD of Chasing Arrow. Cast again to cancel Artillery mode early.

weaknesses: Rooting himself in his Ultimate makes him a stationary, easy target.

strategy.earlyGame: Due to low mobility and vulnerability to ganks, prioritize safe farming under the tower in the farm lane during the early game. Place Skill 2 mines in bushes to secure vision and anticipate enemy Jungler ambushes.
strategy.midGame: Move together with your Support to contribute to tower sieges and objective securing. Deploy your Ultimate in a safe position that blocks enemy approach routes, applying pressure from a distance.
strategy.lateGame: A fully built Huang Zhong has overwhelming firepower. With proper positioning and protection from allies, his artillery can easily dominate teamfights. Constantly be on alert for the positions of enemy Assassins and Mages.
strategy.teamfight: It is dangerous to deploy your Ultimate as soon as a teamfight begins. First, deal damage with basic attacks, confirm that enemy dive skills or CC have been consumed, and then enter artillery mode in a safe place, such as behind your allied Tank.
strategy.commonMistakes: Deploying the Ultimate to become a stationary turret in an exposed location while enemy Assassins are still alive, making yourself an easy target. Also, not using Skill 2 mines and getting ganked while isolated without vision.

synergy.reason: Provides massive shields and peel to protect the stationary Huang Zhong from enemy divers.
Can distract enemies and chain CC them, creating a perfectly safe zone for Huang Zhong's artillery.

counters.reason: High mobility allows him to poke Huang Zhong from afar and quickly close the gap to burst him down.
Can snipe Huang Zhong from outside his artillery range, dealing massive damage with impunity.

strengths: Unrivaled 1v1 dueling capability thanks to his passive, which boosts single-target damage significantly.
His Ultimate provides massive offensive and defensive stats, allowing him to brute-force his way into the enemy backline.
weaknesses: A strictly melee hero, making him highly susceptible to kiting from agile marksmen and mages.
Vulnerable to percentage-health damage, high armor penetration, and heavy crowd control.
strategy.earlyGame: If jungling, quickly aim for level 4. If played in the clash lane, utilize the recovery from Skill 1 to lane tenaciously. Since his passive deals massive damage to isolated enemies, force favorable 1v1 situations.
strategy.midGame: Once core items are completed, he exhibits unmatched strength in 1v1. Split push to draw enemy attention, and if a small skirmish breaks out, activate the Ultimate to instantly eliminate the enemy carry.
strategy.lateGame: Charging head-on into a group of enemies will result in being chain-CC'd and killed. Play like an Assassin, hiding in bushes and waiting for an opportunity to ambush the enemy backline (Marksman or Mage).
strategy.teamfight: Avoid engaging from the front in teamfights; aim to flank from the sides or rear. Once allies initiate combat, activate your Ultimate to dive into the enemy formation, combining Flash and Skill 2 to instantly assassinate the enemy carry.
strategy.commonMistakes: Forcing a fight while the Ultimate is on cooldown. Also, attacking densely grouped enemies where the passive (bonus damage against a single target) cannot be utilized, leading to a lack of damage.

synergy.reason: Ming's attack and movement speed buffs enhance Kaizer's single-target burst and chasing potential.
Provides shields and CC, covering Kaizer's weaknesses in durability and mobility.

counters.reason: Can continuously kite and deal damage while keeping Kaizer at bay in a 1v1 scenario.
His suppressive Ultimate can lock Kaizer down before he gets a chance to wreak havoc.

passive.name: Frenzy Prelude
passive.description: Attacks with his chain sickle. Hitting enemies at maximum range deals an extra 56 (+33% Physical Attack) physical damage and slows them by 15%. Also grants Xuance 15% Movement Speed for 0.75-1.5s.

skill1.name: Divine Hook
skill1.description: Dashes in the designated direction and empowers his next basic attack to deal an extra 4% Physical Attack as physical damage (+0.05% per 10 Bonus Physical Attack). skill2.name: Nightmare Hook
skill2.description: Swings his sickle to charge it up, then throws it. A longer charge increases the hook's range. He gains 30% Movement Speed while charging. The hook deals 320 (+140% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in its path and briefly roots them. It returns upon reaching max distance, applying a Hunting Mark to the first enemy hit. Xuance deals 10% more damage to the marked target and takes 15% less damage from them. Casting this again while a target is marked consumes the mark to toss the enemy behind him, dealing 320 (+140% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage. Deals 50% extra damage to monsters. Canceling the charge reduces the cooldown by 50%. If the marked target dies or is caught by Hook Strike, the cooldown is reduced by 40%.

skill3.name: Hook Strike
skill3.description: Dashes behind the target hooked by Nightmare Hook, dealing 420 (+200% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in his path. If the target is far away, he uses the chain to pull himself forward. If no target is hooked, he dashes near a target and strikes with the hook, dealing 200 (+100% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage. Takes 50% less damage while dashing. Passive: Getting a kill or assist grants 30% Movement Speed (decays) and 25% Attack Speed for 5s (stacks up to 3 times). Hook Strike stores a charge every 6s (max 3 charges).

Ranged basic attacks provide high sustained damage, and his passive makes him an unstoppable chaser after a kill.
weaknesses: Completely reliant on hitting his hook; missing it leaves him very vulnerable. Very high mechanical difficulty.
Weak against crowd control and can severely punish his own team if he accidentally pulls in an enemy tank.
strategy.earlyGame: Because he has very strong ganking potential, clear the jungle quickly to reach level 4 and actively intervene in lanes. Hook enemies with Skill 2 and throw them towards your allies to reliably secure kills or assists.
strategy.midGame: The goal is to accumulate kills and widen the advantage. In small skirmishes, the accuracy of your hook decides everything, so aim carefully. Utilize the passive buff gained upon securing a kill to instantly dominate.
strategy.lateGame: As enemies start moving in groups, it becomes difficult to land hooks on isolated targets. Refrain from engaging head-on and shift to a roaming playstyle, waiting for allies to initiate.
strategy.teamfight: Never be the first to charge in during teamfights. Observe the allied engagement from the outside and land Skill 2 on squishy targets in vulnerable positions. Take down at least one person to trigger the "Frenzy" passive, then wipe out the remaining enemies in one go.
strategy.commonMistakes: Hooking an enemy Tank with Skill 2 but charging in anyway, only to be counterattacked. Also, after missing the hook, trying to fight using only basic attacks and getting killed due to a lack of mobility and damage.

synergy.reason: His guaranteed suppression locks targets down, making it extremely easy for Xuance to land his hook.
Provides CC cleanse and damage reduction, ensuring Xuance isn't chain-stunned when diving the enemy team.

counters.reason: Can instantly shield and protect his allies from anywhere, perfectly disrupting Xuance's assassination attempts.
His AoE silence and explosive burst damage can burst Xuance down before he can execute his combo.

Passive: Moving near terrain or within the device's vision grants camouflage, increasing Movement Speed and Attack Speed by 15%. The Attack Speed bonus persists for 3s after leaving camouflage.

skill2.name: Wild Wind
skill2.description: Aims and fires a shot in the designated direction, dealing 750 (+250% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage plus 8% of the target's Max HP as physical damage. Also heavily slows the target for 0.5s. Takes 2s to fully aim; firing early may cause the shot to miss. Stores up to 3 bullets. Each basic attack hit reduces the charge time by 4%. At 40%/80%/120%/160% Attack Speed, aiming time is reduced by 0.25/0.5/0.75/1s, and the weapon stow time is also reduced.

skill3.name: Escape
skill3.description: Jumps backward and fires in the designated direction, dealing 750 (+180% Bonus Physical Attack) physical damage and slowing enemies by 15% for 2s. Upon landing, gains 15% Movement Speed for 2s. Shares bullets with Wild Wind and consumes 1 upon casting. Basic attacks or damage skills cancel the movement speed boost. Passive: Leveling this skill increases basic attack range by 15.

weaknesses: Over-reliance on sniping reduces team sustained damage; missing shots makes him a liability.

counters.reason: Her physical immunity skill allows her to completely nullify Shouyue's massive sniper damage.
Can instantly shield Shouyue's sniper targets across the map, preventing crucial cross-map kills.

passive.name: Vitality Stone
passive.description: Basic attacks deal magical damage. Skill hits apply a Yin stack. At 4 stacks, a Go piece spawns at the enemy's feet, dealing 100 (+10% Magical Attack) magical damage and permanently granting Yixing 1 Go piece. He gains 5 pieces per kill/assist and every 60s. Each piece grants 1 Magical Attack. Pieces generated by Yin stacks are drawn to the center of the board, dealing 170 (+11% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in their path. Upon taking fatal damage, he becomes ethereal, spawning a piece at his feet, ignoring all damage for 1s, and gaining 30% Movement Speed (150s CD). Consecutive hits from Yin pieces deal 50% damage.

skill1.description: Places a Go piece at the target location, gaining vision of the area for 3s and dealing 100 (+18% Magical Attack) magical damage. Also reduces Respond's CD by 0.5s. Reaching 50/110/181 pieces evolves the empowered attack, adding scatter, +17% damage, and +34% damage, while granting a massive Movement Speed boost for 5s. Stores 1 piece every 6s, up to 2. Subsequent hits from drawn pieces within 0.5s deal 50% damage.

skill2.name: Respond
skill2.description: Forms a Go board at the target area, dealing 160 (+25% Magical Attack) magical damage. The board lasts 10s. Enemies in the center are pulled to the center, taking an additional 160 (+25% Magical Attack) magical damage. If Yixing or enemy heroes pass through the board's edge, they gain a Yin stack, and Yixing gains 50% Movement Speed (decays) for 1s. This triggers once per target. The board disappears if he moves too far away. Deals 180% extra damage to non-hero units.

skill3.name: Tianyuan
skill3.description: Draws a huge ethereal Go board, taking 1.5s to fully form. Enemies inside are knocked up for 0.5s. The board lasts 4s, during which enemies cannot leave its boundaries. The board applies 3 Yin stacks to enemy heroes continuously and grants vision. Catching enemy heroes stores 1 charge of Play. A smaller board reduces the CD by up to 25%. While drawing, Yixing gains 30%-70% Movement Speed (decays).

weaknesses: Lacks solo burst damage; highly dependent on allied follow-up to secure kills.

strategy.midGame: Utilize the massive area of your Ultimate to divide enemies or cut off their retreat routes during contests for the Tyrant or Overlord. Coordinate with allies to trap enemies inside the board and wipe them out.
strategy.lateGame: Despite having high survivability due to his passive's fatal damage immunity (invincibility), he remains a squishy Mage. Poke safely from the backline with go piece combos while waiting for an opportunity to drop a fatal Ultimate.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, deploy your Ultimate in sync with allied CC or position it to block enemy escape routes. After deploying the board, repeatedly cast Skill 1 and Skill 2 inside it to continuously deal AoE damage and slows.
strategy.commonMistakes: Panicking and deploying the Ultimate when enemies still have mobility skills or Flash, trapping no one. Overestimating the invincibility of the passive, stepping too far into the enemy formation, and getting CC-chained to death.

Can easily dive into Yixing's trapped targets to unleash massive burst and clean up the fight.

counters.reason: Exceptional mobility allows him to dodge Yixing's board completely and assassinate him in the backline.

Tapping the skill again or reaching the end of the path makes him dive toward the destination, dealing 150 (+30% Physical Attack) (+30% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in a wide area and knocking them up for 0.75s. If canceled early, the CD is reduced.

weaknesses: Extremely squishy and reliant on perfect mechanical execution and reaction times.

strategy.midGame: Always stick with a fed ally to create 2v1 or 3v2 situations and wreak havoc on the map. When attacked by enemies, switch to the defense buff with Skill 2 depending on the situation, prioritizing your ally's survival.
strategy.lateGame: Since enemy firepower scales up, Ming himself will be instantly killed if he steps forward. Stick tightly behind your allied carry, maintaining the link at all times while waiting for the right moment to provide massive healing with the Ultimate.
strategy.teamfight: Positioning is everything in teamfights. Stay slightly behind your allied carry to avoid getting caught in enemy AoE attacks. The moment your ally's HP drops to a dangerous level, activate your Ultimate to provide a clutch heal.
strategy.commonMistakes: Losing track of your linked target in a teamfight and accidentally buffing the Tank or yourself. Delaying the Ultimate heal and letting your allied carry die. Stepping too far forward and being the first to die.

synergy.reason: This allows you to further enhance the range and damage of your normal attacks with links, allowing you to annihilate enemies from a safe position.
By adding a buff to the highly mobile Marksman, it will be easier to snowball from the early stages.

counters.reason: Due to high mobility and burst damage, there is a risk that all linked allies will be instantly killed.
This is because the instantaneous burst damage is high, and your allies will be defeated before Ming can recover.

passive.name: Passive: Good Omens
passive.description:
Every 5 seconds, the next normal attack deals an additional 238 (70 + 100% of physical attack + 20% of magical attack) magic damage. In addition, absorbs the enemy's HP and recovers 225 HP (225 + 40% of magic attack) for itself and allies linked to the tool, and recovers 4% of additional HP for itself.
When hitting a non-hero enemy, the amount of healing will be reduced to 50%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Time - Carefree
skill1.description:
Throws a ritual tool in the specified direction and links it with an ally. If it does not hit, it will automatically link with an ally hero within range, increasing the target's main attack stat by 20 (20 + 10% of magic attack) (main attack stat 1 = physical attack 1 or magic attack 2) and attack speed by 10%. If hit, increase the target's main attack stats by an additional 25% (decrease over 4 seconds).
Enemies touched by the soul chain take 30 (30 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds.
Passive: Increases own physical attack by 30, magic attack by 65, and movement speed by 7.5%.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Mastery - Glide
skill2.description:
Throws a ritual tool in the specified direction and links it with the enemy. If it does not hit, it will automatically link with enemy heroes within range and reduce the target's physical defense and magic defense by 10%. If hit, additionally reduces the target's physical defense and magic defense by 25% (reduction over 4 seconds).
Deals 30 (30 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds to enemies touched by the soul chain.
Obtain enemy vision linked to soul chains.
Passive: Increases own physical defense and magic defense by 15%.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Peace - Longevity
skill3.description:
After chanting for 0.75 seconds, deal true damage to the enemy linked to the ritual tool and recover HP of allies.
If the tool is linked to one target, consumes 600 HP (600 + 125% of magic attack) to deal the same amount of true damage to the linked enemy or restore the same amount of HP to an ally hero.
If the tool is linked to two targets, absorbs 600 HP (600 + 125% of magic attack) from the enemy and restores HP to allies.
If the linked target is destroyed while chanting, 70% of the cooldown will be returned. The HP consumed by Soul Seer will gradually recover over 5 seconds.

strengths: All skills can be used from level 1, no mana required, and jungle control in the early stages is very high.
You can use long-range poke in human form and high mobility and burst firepower in tiger form.
Utilizing the wall-passing skill of the tiger form, you can quickly move around the map and powerfully support each lane.
weaknesses: If you can't build up an advantage early on, your influence will be lower in late team fights compared to other scaling heroes.
Since it does not have strong HardCC, it is highly dependent on support from allies to restrain the target.
It is necessary to perfectly manage the two forms and energy, and it is easy to be defeated if you switch at the wrong time.
strategy.earlyGame: Because he can use both human and tiger forms from level 1, his early game combat power is top-tier among Junglers. Coordinate with allies to aggressively invade the enemy jungle, steal buffs, and apply immense pressure right from the start.
strategy.midGame: Utilize your high mobility to dash across the map, repeatedly ganking and securing objectives like the Tyrant, focusing all your efforts on widening the gold gap. The human form's ranged attacks are useful when taking down towers.
strategy.lateGame: Compared to other pure carry heroes, his late-game damage scaling falls off. Avoid head-on brawls; poke in human form, and play as a true Assassin by using the tiger form to reliably execute isolated or low-HP enemies.
strategy.teamfight: When a teamfight begins, first chip away at the enemy frontline's HP from a safe distance using human form Skill 1 and basic attacks. Do not miss the opportunity when enemy CC is consumed and their backline's HP is low; switch to tiger form and dive in for kills.
strategy.commonMistakes: Charging directly into the enemy formation in tiger form right as the teamfight starts, taking concentrated fire, and dying instantly. Failing to create an advantage in the early game, merely farming, and losing presence from the mid game onwards.

synergy.reason: This is because it provides strong CC and defense buffs from the early stages and can support Pei's aggressive jungle invasion.
Poke from a distance will reduce the enemy's health, making it easier for Pei to collect kills in tiger form.

counters.reason: With high mobility and invincibility time, you can avoid Pei's burst, and on the other hand, you are likely to be attacked.
Pei's kill potential can be significantly lowered with health rewind and movement buffs in team fights.

forms.name: Skill 3: Human Form

passive.description: When a weapon hits an enemy, it recovers its own energy (7 for normal attacks, 10 for skills. If it hits a non-hero, the amount recovered is halved). When an enhanced skill is activated, it automatically consumes 30 energy. After leaving the battle, energy gradually decreases.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Electrifying Charge

Swings your weapon twice, dealing 80 (80 + 100% of physical damage) physical damage to enemies hit each time. Recovers own HP by 30 (30 + 16% of additional physical damage) with each hit.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Force Field Suppression

Slams the ground, dealing 250 (250 + 100% physical damage) physical damage to surrounding enemies and reducing movement speed by 50% for 1.5 seconds. At the same time, gain a shield that absorbs 1000 damage (lasts for 6 seconds).
When enhanced: Damage increases to 375 (375 + 150% of physical damage) and shield absorption increases to 1500. Additionally, it absorbs energy from surrounding enemies, recovering 50 HP and increasing movement speed by 20% for 1 second for each enemy hero hit.

strengths: Very powerful in early lane battles and has an advantage against many facing heroes in Crash Lane.
He has a high HP recovery ability with skills and has excellent sustain that allows him to stay in the lane for a long time.
Skills with accumulated energy have enhanced damage and CC, and exhibit high burst firepower.
weaknesses: Combat power is highly dependent on the energy gauge, and if you engage in a battle with insufficient energy, you will not be able to demonstrate your true power.
Although it has durability, it is weak to enemy CC chains and is easily incapacitated if its skill combo is interrupted.
It is difficult to get close to highly mobile carries and poke-composed enemies who fight at a distance.
strategy.earlyGame: A fully energized Biron is extremely powerful. Take control of the clash lane by attacking minions to build energy and landing the enhanced Skill 1 to simultaneously deal damage and heal.
strategy.midGame: Utilize your high sustain to push the lane while invading the enemy jungle or roaming to the mid lane. In small skirmishes, use the knock-up CC from your enhanced Skill 2 to set up kills.
strategy.lateGame: Function as a frontline bruiser who exerts massive pressure on enemy carries. Since energy management decides victories, it is mandatory to attack nearby monsters or minions to secure energy before entering combat.
strategy.teamfight: Starting with 30 or more energy, combine Flash and enhanced Skill 2 to dash onto the enemy backline and instantly apply CC. Immediately after, deploy your Ultimate to gain a massive shield, running rampant in the enemy formation while healing with Skill 1.
strategy.commonMistakes: Diving into a teamfight with zero energy, meaning skills are not enhanced and you get killed due to a lack of damage, CC, and healing. Trying to approach the backline without Flash and getting kited endlessly from afar.

synergy.reason: Because stealth and strong pull make it easier for Biron's skills and area damage to hit multiple people.
Because you can safely support Biron's forceful dive with the shield and teleport from ult.

counters.reason: Biron's strong shield offsets Biron's damage, giving him an advantageous trade in lane battles.
This is because the guaranteed damage penetrates Biron's reliable shield and causes great damage.

passive.name: Passive: Deer Spirit
passive.description: When you receive a Hard CC effect such as knockup, knockback, or traction, you will transform into a deer for 4 seconds (activated once every 60 seconds). While in deer form, you cannot attack or be attacked, and reduce the movement speed of enemies around you by 30%.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Wandering Beam
skill1.description:
Fires an energy bullet, dealing 250 (250 + 30% of magic damage) magic damage to enemies along the route. If it detects an enemy hero before reaching the end point, it will track the enemy, dealing magic damage, knocking it up for 0.5 seconds, and gaining vision for 5 seconds.
Possessed state: Tracking range increases by 20%.
Deer state: quickly returns to human form.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Accelerating Blitz
skill2.description:
Automatically attacks up to 3 enemies (prioritizing heroes) for 5 seconds, dealing 200 (200 + 30% of magic damage) magic damage each time. While active, normal attacks cannot be performed.
Possessed: Attack range increases by 20%.
Deer state: Jump forward.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Verdant Protector
skill3.description:
Possesses another allied hero and grants a shield that absorbs all damage for 750 (750 + 50% of magic damage + 10% of maximum HP). When possessed, the CD of all skills is reduced by 30%. When the shield is destroyed, the possessed state is removed and the character enters Cooldown. If you remove possession from yourself, 50% of your cooldown will be returned.
Deer status: Gives nearby allies a movement speed increase effect.

strengths: Possess allies (especially Assassins and Marksmen) to provide powerful shields and buffs to maximize their survivability.
Has mini-stun and knockback from targeted tracking bullets, and has excellent peel ability to protect allies.
His survivability is extremely high due to his passive ability to turn into a deer that cannot be targeted when receiving Hard CC.
weaknesses: It is very weak in the early stages until you reach level 4 and learn the ultimate, and its influence in lane battles is low.
It is a passive support that is completely dependent on allies, and cannot demonstrate its true value in a configuration without a strong carry or vanguard tank.
Since it cannot set up a front line by itself, it is vulnerable to high firepower and high durability configurations that quickly destroy shields.
strategy.earlyGame: In the early game, stay beside your allied Marksman, helping them clear minions while safely securing vision. Since she is extremely fragile until level 4, avoid forced engagements.
strategy.midGame: After unlocking the Ultimate, possess the most fed carry on your team (Jungler or Marksman) and move around the map. It is crucial to use Skill 1 to check bushes for vision and prevent ambushes.
strategy.lateGame: The shield amount in the late game becomes very large, but since enemy firepower is also high, mastering the technique of manually ending the possession before the shield breaks to reduce the cooldown is essential.
strategy.teamfight: In teamfights, intentionally taking an enemy's fatal CC skill to trigger the passive deer form and bait out their skills is a powerful tactic. Quickly return to human form afterward and possess an ally again to provide shields.
strategy.commonMistakes: A common beginner mistake is "parasitic play" where you possess an ally and do nothing. You must not just wait for the shield to break; you need to dismount at the right time to manage the cooldown, and actively poke with basic attacks and skills.

synergy.reason: She has high mobility, and Yaria's shield and CC perfectly support her ult dive.
He is a highly mobile Assassin, and when possessed by Yaria, he is able to perform powerful ganks that ignore terrain.

counters.reason: Due to the shield-effective passive and long range, Yaria's strong point, the shield, can be easily stripped away.
Due to the overwhelming burst damage, the possessed ally will be blown away along with the shield.

passive.name: Passive: Cloud God's Wings
passive.description: Enters battle in flight, increases movement speed by 50, and can move ignoring terrain. Normal attacks become melee attacks that deal physical damage equal to 4% of maximum HP (+1% additional physical damage). Damaging an enemy while flying increases movement speed by 15 and defense penetration by 5% (up to 3 stacks). When passing through terrain, the flight state persists.

skill1.description: Gradually increases your movement speed for 2 seconds (up to 150%). If you hold it down, the movement speed increase effect will continue. When you release your finger, you will charge in the specified direction, dealing 200 (200 + 60% of additional physical damage) physical damage to the enemy and stunning them for 0.5 seconds. After charging, move behind the target and remove your own movement speed increase effect.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Ruoying - Dance of Flowers
skill2.description: Fires feather blades around you, dealing 200 (200 + 50% of additional physical damage) physical damage to the enemy. Enemies hit have their movement speed reduced by 30% for 1 second. While active, damage taken by yourself is reduced by 30%.

Then, it swoops down and attacks the enemy, dealing 350 (350 + 100% of additional physical damage) physical damage and knocking it up for 0.5 seconds. After plummeting, it rains feather blades, dealing 100 (100 + 20% of additional physical damage) physical damage to enemies within the area per second and reducing movement speed by 30% for 1 second.

strengths: It has overwhelming mobility that allows it to fly ignoring the terrain, and is good at surprise attacks and jungle invasions from unpredictable routes.
Utilizing his mobility, he has extremely high map pressure in the early stages, and can always force enemy laners to be on guard.
Excellent ability to quickly dive into the enemy's rear guard (Marksman or Mage) and instantly deal burst damage and kill them.
weaknesses: Its combat power is completely dependent on its flight status, and it will be greatly weakened if it takes too much damage or lands out of energy.
The Assassin's fate is that it has low durability, and if it gets caught by CC when diving into the enemy's territory, it is likely to be defeated without being able to escape.
In group battles in the final stages, enemies form dense formations, making it difficult to kill them alone and reducing their influence.
strategy.earlyGame: Utilize his flight ability, which ignores terrain, to invade the enemy jungle and steal resources from the early game, or set up ganks from unpredictable routes to create an advantage.
strategy.midGame: Use your mobility to traverse the entire map and actively hunt down isolated enemy Marksmen or Mages. In small skirmishes, you can use the invincibility frames of the Ultimate to execute tower dives.
strategy.lateGame: In the late game when enemies group up, charging head-on will result in instant death. Wait for an allied Tank to initiate and for enemy CC to be consumed before diving onto the backline.
strategy.teamfight: Your role in teamfights is strictly "assassinating the backline" and "cleaning up after the fight." Managing your energy to maintain flight during combat is vital; if the gauge is about to deplete, you need the composure to briefly disengage and recover.
strategy.commonMistakes: Neglecting energy management for the flight state and reverting to walking right in front of enemies is fatal. Also, being the very first to dive into a teamfight and taking concentrated fire is a common NG move for beginners.

synergy.reason: Because he can restrain the enemy with strong CC and ensure Yunzhongjun's dive into the back line.
To increase Yunzhongjun's survivability and kill line with shields and additional CC to help him safely escape.

counters.reason: This is because you can forcibly shoot down Yunzhongjun, who flies in while in flight, using target suppression CC.
This is because you can restrain Yunzhongjun who dives without missing with a guaranteed suppression CC and lead to a kill.

strengths: You can use either light form (for long-distance poke and group battles) and dark form (for close combat and split push) depending on the situation.
1v1 performance in dark form and tower destruction speed are extremely high, allowing pressure to be applied to the entire map.
In light form, it has high burst damage and wide-range attacks, and can quickly whittle down the enemy's health from medium range.
weaknesses: It is difficult to operate as it is necessary to accurately understand the strengths and weaknesses of each form and use maneuvers and combos according to the situation.
Both forms are weak against heavy Hard CC, making it difficult to deal with compositions that have a wealth of enemy interference skills.
The dark form is not suitable for 5v5 group battles, and if you do not keep pace with your allies, there is a risk of being isolated and defeated individually.
strategy.earlyGame: He cannot change forms until level 4 and is extremely weak, so endure by safely clearing minions under the tower. Avoid enemy harass while reliably securing experience and gold.
strategy.midGame: Switch between Light form and Dark form depending on the situation. Dark form excels in split pushing and 1v1s, while Light form excels in poking and teamfight damage. Determine which form to main based on team compositions and the flow of the match.
strategy.lateGame: If in Dark form, thoroughly split push side lanes to draw enemy attention; if in Light form, continuously output overwhelming AoE damage with skills from behind your allies. Careful positioning is required, as a mistake in placement leads to immediate death.
strategy.teamfight: In Light form teamfights, utilize the super armor and damage reduction while charging skills, and drop your Ultimate and Skill 2 into enemy clusters. If participating in teamfights in Dark form, you must play like an Assassin, targeting squishy enemies from the flanks or rear.
strategy.commonMistakes: Misunderstanding the characteristics of the forms, such as charging head-on into a teamfight in Dark form or isolating yourself to 1v1 in Light form. Also, since form changing takes time, avoid switching in a vulnerable state during combat.

synergy.reason: Stealth and pull make it easier to reliably hit multiple enemies with Light Li Xin's powerful ranged ult.
With recovery and CC, it safely supports Dark Li Xin's approach and Light Li Xin's poke.

counters.reason: Because you can easily block Li Xin's burst damage and approach with a strong shield and knockback.
This is because his timer ability in the final stages is extremely high, and he can stop Yami Li Xin's strength, the split push.

skill1.description:

When you self-disappear the magic circle, your movement speed increases by 20% and your critical rate increases by 20% for 3 seconds.

strengths: It has an overwhelming normal attack range that is the longest in the game, allowing you to attack enemies unilaterally from a safe position.
Because the skill has special shield effect (50% damage increase), it is very strong against tanks and supports that use shields frequently.
The firepower in the final stage when all the items are available is tremendous, and if you can handle it properly, you will have overwhelming carry power that will lead your team to victory.
weaknesses: It is a glass cannon with no movement skills or self-defense skills, making it extremely vulnerable when approached by enemy Assassins.
Firepower is low in the early stages, and it takes a lot of time and gold to complete core items.
Skills and enhanced normal attacks consume a lot of mana, so if you neglect mana management, you will run out of breath at critical moments.
strategy.earlyGame: Because her early game damage is modest and she lacks mobility, do not stray far from the tower and farm safely. Utilize the range of Skill 1 to last-hit minions from a position where enemies cannot counterattack.
strategy.midGame: Once core items are assembled, her damage spikes. Always stick with allied Supports and Tanks, poking from a safe backline to create advantages during objective contests (Tyrant and Overlord).
strategy.lateGame: A full-build Garo has overwhelming firepower and range. You can unilaterally attack from outside the enemy's range, melting even shielded enemy Tanks in an instant. Never approach unwarded areas of the map alone.
strategy.teamfight: Positioning in teamfights is everything. Since she has no self-defense skills, always stay at the very back of your team. Deploy the Ultimate's magic circle to boost your critical rate, and take down enemies in your line of sight one by one.
strategy.commonMistakes: Continuing to use basic attacks with Skill 1 active and completely depleting mana is a common beginner mistake. Also, stepping too far forward without checking the minimap for enemy Assassins will result in them instantly closing the gap and killing you.

synergy.reason: With firepower and recovery buffs, Garo's overwhelming range and firepower can be further enhanced and snowballed.
With his powerful shield and peel ability, he can protect Garo, who doesn't have escape skills, from the enemy Assassin.

counters.reason: This is because it uses its high mobility to sneak through Garo's range, quickly closing the distance and dealing burst damage.
This is because the strong burst damage from stealth can instantly kill Garo even if there is an escort.
